scenario.name: Webhook Burst Triage
scenario.hypothesis: Bursty inbound events should reveal whether arms can deduplicate, prioritize, and avoid over-coordinating under load.
scenario.expectedDivergence: single may process linearly; central should delegate independent incidents; choreography should show durable claims preventing duplicate work.
scenario.taskPacket:
# Agent Coordination Patterns Task Packet

This packet is self-contained. Do not inspect the repository, shell, or filesystem.
Use only the Firegrid session and coordination-channel tools exposed to you.

Scenario: Webhook Burst Triage

Task:
- Watch coordination.work for inbound webhook incidents.
- Wait for these inbound workIds on coordination.work before finalizing: linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-102:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate, github:check:failed.
- Use scalar matches such as match.workId for those waits; do not guess payload timestamps.
- Group duplicate incidents by external entity.
- Triage severity and publish findings.
- The final artifact must list the deduped incident groups and which evidence rows supported each group.

Output contract:
- Produce one concise final artifact with body under 1,200 characters.
- State which evidence you inspected.
- State the implementation or design recommendation.
- State open questions.
- Do not claim coordination helped unless the artifacts show why.

inboundSignals.title: linear.issue.updated TF-101
inboundSignals.body: Issue TF-101 changed labels from bug to regression.

inboundSignals.title: linear.issue.updated TF-102
inboundSignals.body: Issue TF-102 received a customer escalation comment.

inboundSignals.title: linear.issue.updated TF-101 duplicate
inboundSignals.body: Duplicate delivery for TF-101 with the same external event id.

inboundSignals.title: github.check.failed
inboundSignals.body: CI failed for a related branch; failure signature mentions unknown-channel.

finalArtifact.title: Webhook Burst Triage — deduped incident groups
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected (4 coordination.work rows):
- linear:TF-101:a — linear.issue.updated TF-101 (labels bug→regression, atMs 0)
- linear:TF-101:duplicate — duplicate delivery, same external entity TF-101 (atMs 500)
- linear:TF-102:a — linear.issue.updated TF-102, customer escalation comment (atMs 250)
- github:check:failed — CI failure on related branch, signature mentions unknown-channel (atMs 1500)

Deduped incident groups by external entity:
1) linear/TF-101 (sev: high — regression label) — rows: linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate (duplicate suppressed by workId/external id).
2) linear/TF-102 (sev: high — customer escalation) — rows: linear:TF-102:a.
3) github/check (sev: medium — CI red, unknown-channel signature; likely correlates to TF-101 regression but no explicit link) — rows: github:check:failed.

Recommendation: dedupe webhooks by (source, externalEntityId, externalEventId) at ingest; bucket downstream triage per entity. Severity ladder: customer escalation ≥ regression-label > CI-red. Treat unknown-channel CI failure as a correlation hint, not a merge key.

Open questions: is the duplicate redelivery from provider retry or a replay-storm bug? Should github/check link to TF-101 via branch metadata? Confirm dedupe window (workId-stable vs time-bounded).

Coordination value: single-arm — coordination board only used to receive inputs via scalar workId waits; no multi-agent claim/finding traffic was needed.

finalArtifact.title: Webhook burst triage — central orchestrator
finalArtifact.body:
Deduped incident groups (by external entity):

1) linear:TF-101 — severity P2 (label flip bug→regression). Evidence rows: linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate (same external event id, deduped).
2) linear:TF-102 — severity P1 (customer escalation comment). Evidence rows: linear:TF-102:a.
3) github:check:failed — severity P1 (CI failure; signature mentions 'unknown-channel'). Evidence rows: github:check:failed. Correlation hint: 'unknown-channel' signature is a known channel-routing failure class but no direct linkage to TF-101/TF-102 from the evidence given.

Evidence inspected: 4 coordination.work rows above + child analyst replies.

Children created: Analyst-A (Linear lane) confirmed TF-101 dedup pair + TF-102 singleton; Analyst-B (GitHub lane) flagged check:failed with unknown-channel hint. Their replies shaped the dedup grouping and severities.

Recommendation: dedup webhook handlers by (source, external entity id) before triage; route P1 customer-escalation + CI failures to oncall; treat regression-label flips as P2 review.

Open questions: Is the 'unknown-channel' CI failure linked to either Linear ticket? Need a correlator over branch→ticket. Did orchestration help here? Marginally — only 1 of 4 work rows had ambiguity needing a specialist; lane split added overhead vs payoff.

finalArtifact.title: Webhook burst triage — deduped groups + recommendation
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected (coordination.work rowIds):
- 520c320d linear:TF-101:a — TF-101 label bug→regression
- 60be49da linear:TF-101:duplicate — same external id as TF-101:a
- 80d09943 linear:TF-102:a — TF-102 customer escalation
- c8458570 github:check:failed — CI failed, signature mentions 'unknown-channel'

Deduped groups:
G1 Linear TF-101 regression — rows [520c320d, 60be49da]. Suppress :duplicate; canonical=TF-101:a. Severity: medium.
G2 Linear TF-102 escalation — rows [80d09943]. Severity: high (customer escalation).
G3 GitHub CI failure — rows [c8458570]. Severity: medium.

Recommendation: dedup by external-entity id (linear issue key) + delivery id at the webhook ingestor; do not merge G3 into G1 on source proximity alone — require a signature match. Triage order: G2 → G1 → G3.

Open questions:
1. Does the github 'unknown-channel' signature reference the same code path as TF-101's regression? Need a confirming evidence row before merging G3 into G1.
2. Is the duplicate delivery a webhook-provider retry or an at-least-once replay from our edge? Affects whether dedup belongs at ingestor or downstream.

Coordination value: peers (planner/builder/reviewer) are spawned and may publish their own claim/finding/review/final rows on the board; this final reflects the conductor-peer slice only — additional peer artifacts are expected on the channels.
